question.text: The SEBI (Alternative Investment Funds) Regulations were introduced in which year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SEBI (Alternative Investment Funds) Regulations were introduced in 2012. They replaced the earlier SEBI (Venture Capital Funds) Regulations 1996 and provided a comprehensive framework for all types of alternative investment funds in India. **The Trap:** 2012 is a critical year for the AIF industry. Don't confuse it with 1996 (VCF Regulations), 2008 (global financial crisis), or 2015 (NIIF establishment). **Cross-Exam Context:** This is one of the most fundamental facts tested across all XIX-series exams. The 2012 regulations form the bedrock of all AIF-related questions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 2012 AIF Regulations represent a watershed moment — they transformed India's fragmented VC/PE landscape into a structured, regulated AIF industry. This regulatory maturity is a key selling point when compared to less regulated alternative investment markets.
